description: Video recommendation algorithm based on readiness assessment results for Chapter 10: Elephants, Tigers, and Leopards (Data Interpretation with Wildlife)

excellent_performance.rationale: Student has excellent number comparison and table-reading skills, can fast-track to charts and conservation math with challenge content

videoSequence.reason: Introduction to reading wildlife data tables
Reading bar graphs and charts with animal data
Pictographs and picture graphs with wildlife
Challenge: Conservation math using real data

ready_performance.rationale: Student is ready, provide complete coverage of data interpretation concepts

videoSequence.reason: Introduction to reading wildlife data tables
Reading bar graphs with animal data
Conservation math with real wildlife data

partial_readiness.rationale: Student has gaps in number comparison or table reading, needs targeted review before data interpretation

adaptiveLogic.description: Select prerequisite videos based on specific weak concepts

exampleSequence.reason: Review: Comparing numbers with greater than and less than
Review: Reading information from rows and columns
Review: Addition and subtraction with larger numbers
Start main topic: Reading wildlife data tables

not_ready.rationale: Student needs significant prerequisite review in number comparison and table reading before attempting data interpretation

videoSequence.reason: Essential: Learn to read rows and columns in tables
Critical: Review addition and subtraction for data problems
Gentle intro to wildlife data tables
Begin main content: Comparing numbers from data

postPlaylistAction.message: After watching these prerequisite videos, retake the readiness quiz to check if you are ready to continue with data interpretation.

conceptBasedRemediation.description: Map specific weak concepts to remedial videos

number_comparison.conceptName: Comparing numbers using greater than, less than, and equal to

reading_tables.conceptName: Reading information from rows and columns in a table

addition_subtraction.conceptName: Addition and subtraction with larger numbers

counting_large.conceptName: Counting and reading large numbers up to thousands

comprehensionCheckpoints.description: Quick checks during video watching to adjust playlist

questions.question: A table shows: Tigers in Park A = 45, Tigers in Park B = 32. Which park has more tigers?
questions.explanation: Park A has 45 tigers and Park B has 32. Since 45 is greater than 32, Park A has more tigers.

questions.question: If a table shows elephants = 1,200 and leopards = 850, how many more elephants are there?
questions.explanation: 1,200 - 850 = 350. There are 350 more elephants than leopards.

ifAllCorrect.message: Excellent! You can read data tables very well.

ifAllWrong.reason: Student struggling with table reading, review rows and columns

questions.question: In a bar graph, a taller bar means:
questions.explanation: In a bar graph, a taller bar represents a bigger number. The taller the bar, the more there are!

ifCorrect.reason: Student ready for conservation math challenge

ifWrong.message: Watch the bar graph video again. Remember: taller bars mean bigger numbers!

skip_ahead.action: Skip practice video, jump to conservation math or next topic

add_support.action: Pause main topic, insert prerequisite review on number comparison and table reading

suggest_break.trigger: After 4 videos watched in a single session
suggest_break.action: Suggest taking a 5-minute break before continuing

recommend_practice.trigger: Student completes videos but scores < 70% on checkpoint
recommend_practice.action: Recommend practising reading tables and comparing numbers with real examples at home

recommend_retake.trigger: Student in not_ready category completes all prerequisite videos
recommend_retake.action: Strongly recommend retaking readiness assessment before main content

high_score.message: Excellent! You are great at comparing numbers and reading tables. You are ready to explore data about elephants, tigers, and leopards. We have created a fast-track playlist with challenge content for you.

good_score.message: Good work! You understand numbers and tables well. Let's explore wildlife data about elephants, tigers, and leopards with complete coverage of all topics.

partial_score.message: You have some gaps in comparing numbers or reading tables. Don't worry! We will start with targeted review videos on the concepts you need, then move to wildlife data.

low_score.message: You need to review number comparison and table reading first. We have created a playlist focused on building a strong foundation. After watching, you can retake the quiz and then explore wildlife data.

conceptProgressionMap.description: Shows how data interpretation concepts build on each other

playlistCompletion.description: % of students who watch >= 80% of recommended videos

practiceQuizPassRate.description: % of students who score >= 70% on practice quiz after watching playlist

videoRelevanceRating.description: Average student rating (out of 5) for playlist helpfulness

timeToMastery.description: Average minutes from readiness quiz to practice quiz pass

tableReadingFluency.description: % of students who can correctly read data from a 2-way table within 15 seconds

graphInterpretation.description: % of students who can correctly answer a question from a bar graph or pictograph

implementationNotes.recommendedValidation: Test with 15-20 Grade 4 students across all readiness levels

expectedOutcomes.excellent_students: Complete in 40-50 minutes with 90-100% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.ready_students: Complete in 35-40 minutes with 80-90% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.partial_students: Complete in 50-55 minutes with 70-80% practice quiz score (after prerequisite review)
expectedOutcomes.not_ready_students: Require prerequisite review (25 min) + retake + main content (25 min) = 50-60 total minutes, expected 60-75% on practice quiz

commonChallenges.challenge1: Students reading the wrong row or column in a table
commonChallenges.solution1: Teach the finger-trace method: use one finger along the row and another along the column to find where they meet.
commonChallenges.challenge2: Students confusing bar graph height with the label below
commonChallenges.solution2: Emphasise that the height of the bar tells us the number. Always look at the scale on the side.
commonChallenges.challenge3: Students who can read data but cannot compare or calculate with it
commonChallenges.solution3: Practice extracting two numbers from a table and then comparing or adding/subtracting them as a two-step process.
